reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All 3 gates passed. Gate 1: _getAssetPrice has multiple non-test callers in production (BaseAuction.sol L238,315,342,429,764,798; GPV2CompatibleAuction.sol L153). Gate 2: price_feeds is SEMI_TRUSTED but explicitly in_scope_for_bug_bounty=true with a carve-out for PriceManager staleness logic. Gate 3: PriceManager.sol is in-scope; oracle_verification exclusion applies only to cryptographic Data Streams report verification, not fallback feed handling. Spec deviation confirmed at PriceManager.sol L386: feedInfo.usdDataFeed.latestRoundData() is called without try/catch inside the stale-price branch, meaning a reverted fallback AggregatorV3 (deprecated, paused, or circuit-broken) propagates through _getAssetPrice even when withValidation=false, violating the checkUpkeep no-revert soft-skip invariant. Verdict is CONFIRMED_POTENTIAL rather than CONFIRMED_VULNERABILITY because the trigger condition (fallback AggregatorV3 reverting) is an external infrastructure state not directly controlled by an attacker. Severity Medium is appropriate per scope thresholds: DoS of core checkUpkeep functionality matches 'Auction participation blocking (DoS)' example.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: SG-004 — checkUpkeep soft-skip invariant: forall checkUpkeep(asset): ineligible condition => continue (no revert), return false
